text.title: Compliance Checklist by Domain — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Compliance Checklist by Domain must enforce.

rule.statement: Pick only the checklists for the engagement's actual regulatory domains; do not apply all-of-everything.
rule.rationale: Applying irrelevant domains creates audit theatre and dilutes attention from the real items.

rule.statement: Every checklist item cites the underlying regulatory clause (article + paragraph).
rule.rationale: Clauseless items become 'because the auditor said so'; clauses make the requirement falsifiable.

rule.statement: Every item names the evidence-required artefact (log, screenshot, policy doc) before sign-off.
rule.rationale: Items without evidence become tick-the-box drift.

rule.statement: The checklist has a named owner (BA or compliance lead) who reviews quarterly.
rule.rationale: Anonymous checklists rot; owned checklists refresh.

rule.statement: A single-domain pass holds ≤50 items; larger lists are split into sub-domains.
rule.rationale: Lists beyond 50 items get skimmed; bounded size keeps attention high.
